strogonoffDesc: Creamy Brazilian-style beef strips in a rich sauce with mushrooms.
cabbageDesc: A leafy green vegetable used in salads, soups, and fermented dishes.
friedChickenDesc: Golden crispy chicken pieces fried until crunchy outside, juicy inside.
vanillaIceCreamDesc: Classic frozen dessert made from cream and vanilla, smooth and sweet.
cornWithButterDesc: Sweet corn served warm with a generous pat of melted butter.
cremeBruleeDesc: French dessert with silky custard topped by a caramelized sugar crust.
riceDesc: A global staple grain cooked in water, base for countless dishes worldwide.
beansDesc: Protein-rich legumes simmered in broth, a comfort food in many cultures.
veganBurgerDesc: A plant-based patty made from vegetables or legumes, served burger-style.
baconBurgerDesc: A beef patty stacked with crispy bacon, melted cheese, and classic toppings.
milkshakeDesc: Thick blended drink of ice cream and milk, topped with whipped cream.
broccolliPizzaDesc: Pizza topped with fresh broccoli florets, cheese, and tomato sauce.
caesarSaladDesc: Romaine lettuce with Caesar dressing, croutons, and parmesan shavings.
carrotSoupDesc: Smooth warm soup blended from roasted carrots, often spiced with ginger.
beefLasagnaDesc: Layers of pasta, rich meat sauce, béchamel, and melted cheese baked together.
veganLasagnaDesc: A plant-based lasagna with vegetable filling and dairy-free alternatives.
frenchFriesWithCheddarDesc: Golden crispy fries smothered in warm melted cheddar cheese sauce.
sushiDesc: Japanese dish of vinegared rice with fresh fish, seafood, or vegetables.
lionDesc: The king of the jungle — a large African cat with a powerful mane and roar.
polarBearDesc: A massive white bear built for the Arctic, an expert swimmer and seal hunter.
flamingoDesc: A tall pink bird known for standing on one leg and its curved filtering beak.
parrotDesc: A colorful tropical bird famous for mimicking human speech and living in flocks.
alligatorDesc: A large reptile with a broad snout, found in swamps and rivers.
slothDesc: The world's slowest mammal, hanging upside down in South American rainforests.
pantherDesc: A large black cat — actually a melanistic jaguar or leopard in disguise.
antDesc: A tiny but incredibly strong insect living in organized colonies of millions.
ostrichDesc: The world's largest bird — flightless but capable of running at 70 km/h.
zebraDesc: An African wild horse recognized by its distinctive black and white stripes.
octopusDesc: A sea creature with eight arms, three hearts, and color-changing skin.
capybaraDesc: The world's largest rodent — a semi-aquatic animal from South America.
batDesc: The only flying mammal, navigating in complete darkness using echolocation.
whaleSharkDesc: The world's largest fish — a gentle filter feeder up to 12 meters long.
catDesc: A popular domestic pet known for independence, agility, and affectionate behavior.
dogDesc: Man's best friend — a loyal social animal with hundreds of different breeds.
vampireDesc: An undead creature that feeds on blood, repelled by garlic and sunlight.
skeletonDesc: Bony remains of the dead — a classic symbol of Halloween and the underworld.
witchDesc: A spell-caster typically depicted with a pointed hat, broom, and cauldron.
ghostDesc: The spirit of a deceased person lingering in the world, haunting familiar places.
zombieDesc: A reanimated corpse that mindlessly craves human flesh and spreads infection.
werewolfDesc: A human cursed to transform into a wolf-like beast under the full moon.
mummyDesc: An ancient Egyptian preserved corpse wrapped in bandages, cursed to walk again.
blackCatDesc: A mysterious feline long tied to witchcraft, bad luck, and Halloween folklore.
spiderDesc: An eight-legged web-spinning arachnid — a classic symbol of spookiness.
batHalloweenDesc: A nocturnal flying mammal linked to vampires and the darkness of Halloween.
scarecrowDesc: A straw-stuffed human figure from the fields, sometimes given a sinister life.
grimReaperDesc: Death personified — a cloaked skeleton carrying a large scythe.
headlessHorsemanDesc: A terrifying galloping ghost with no head, wielding a flaming pumpkin.
goblinDesc: A small mischievous creature from folklore, known for causing trouble.
gargoyleDesc: A stone monster carved on buildings to ward off evil, said to come alive at night.
demonDesc: A supernatural evil being known for possessing humans and spreading chaos.
trollDesc: A large dim-witted creature from Nordic folklore, living under bridges.
bansheeDesc: An Irish spirit whose wailing scream foretells the death of someone nearby.
ghoulDesc: A dark spirit from Arabian folklore that haunts graves and feeds on the dead.
cyclopsDesc: A giant from Greek mythology with a single enormous eye in its forehead.
medusaDesc: A snake-haired monster from Greek myth whose gaze turns people to stone.
minotaurDesc: A Greek myth creature with a man's body and a bull's head, trapped in a labyrinth.
chocolateCakeDesc: A rich layered dessert of moist sponge, chocolate cream, and glossy ganache frosting.
tacosDesc: Mexican street food — crispy or soft tortillas filled with seasoned meat, salsa, and toppings.
guacamoleDesc: A creamy Mexican dip made from mashed avocados, lime juice, cilantro, and chili.
paellaDesc: A Spanish saffron-infused rice dish cooked with seafood, chicken, and vegetables in one pan.
croissantDesc: A buttery crescent-shaped French pastry with dozens of thin, flaky, laminated layers.
ramenDesc: Japanese noodle soup with a rich broth, topped with a soft egg, pork slices, and green onions.
pancakesDesc: Fluffy flat breakfast cakes stacked high, served warm with maple syrup and melted butter.
grilledSalmonDesc: A fillet of salmon cooked over open heat, with a crispy skin and tender, flaky interior.
cheeseFondueDesc: A Swiss dish of melted cheese kept warm in a communal pot, served with bread for dipping.
hotDogDesc: A cooked sausage nestled in a soft sliced bun, classically topped with mustard and ketchup.
tiramisuDesc: An Italian coffee-flavored dessert with airy mascarpone layers and rum-soaked ladyfingers.
tomatoSoupDesc: A smooth, velvety soup blended from slow-roasted tomatoes, often paired with a grilled cheese sandwich.
elephantDesc: The world's largest land animal, known for its trunk, ivory tusks, and remarkable memory.
penguinDesc: A flightless seabird of the Southern Hemisphere that waddles on land but swims with surprising speed.
tigerDesc: The largest wild cat — a solitary striped ambush predator from the forests of Asia.
dolphinDesc: A highly intelligent marine mammal known for acrobatics, echolocation clicks, and playful social behavior.
giraffeDesc: The tallest animal on Earth, with an impossibly long neck for browsing leaves high in the treetops.
kangarooDesc: An Australian marsupial that carries its joey in a pouch and covers ground in powerful leaps.
chameleonDesc: A slow-moving lizard famous for independently rotating eyes and color-changing skin.
peacockDesc: A bird celebrated for its spectacular iridescent fan-tail covered in vivid eye-shaped feathers.
gorillaDesc: The largest primate — a powerful yet gentle ape that lives in the mountain forests of central Africa.
jellyfishDesc: A boneless, brainless sea creature with a translucent bell and trailing stinging tentacles.
eagleDesc: A powerful bird of prey with telescopic vision and broad wings, a global symbol of freedom.
pandaDesc: A rare bear from China's bamboo forests, instantly recognized by its black and white coat.
crocodileDesc: An ancient armored reptile that floats motionless in rivers before striking with explosive speed.
seahorseDesc: A tiny upright fish unique in the animal kingdom — the male carries and gives birth to the young.
frankensteinDesc: A reanimated monster stitched together from corpse parts, brought to life by a mad scientist and lightning.
jackOLanternDesc: A carved pumpkin lit from within by a candle — the glowing face that defines Halloween night.
krakenDesc: A colossal Norse sea monster said to drag entire ships and their crews down to the ocean floor.
wendigoDesc: A cannibalistic spirit from Algonquin folklore that haunts frozen forests and possesses starving travelers.
doppelgangerDesc: A ghostly double of a living person — spotting your own is considered a dark omen of death.
cerberusDesc: The fearsome three-headed hound standing guard at the gates of the underworld in Greek mythology.
poltergeistDesc: An invisible mischievous spirit known for slamming doors, hurling objects, and terrorizing households.
chimeraDesc: A fire-breathing abomination from Greek myth — lion's head, goat's body, serpent's tail fused into one.
